question: Why might a search for one exact RPC or Horizon endpoint miss the reference page?

golden.answer: Use the RPC/Horizon docs search for structure, migration, error, admin, and method-guide material, then follow official page links or the data skill for procedure. The exposed catalog explicitly says auto-generated per-method RPC and per-endpoint Horizon references are not in this search index; a zero search hit is not evidence that the API does not exist.
keyFacts: Treats the docs-search index as intentionally incomplete for generated per-method/per-endpoint references.
Does not infer API absence from an unindexed search result.
avoid: Do NOT say an RPC or Horizon API is nonexistent solely because this search operation did not return it.
golden.notes: N1 live contract probe 2026-07-11: Catalog note is the stable contract; live probe context was established through the local MCP execute surface.

sources.note: Exposed operation contract and catalog notes.
Committed inventory snapshot for the service family.

corroboration.claim: Treats the docs-search index as intentionally incomplete for generated per-method/per-endpoint references.
